title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

description: Experience the ultimate steakhouse flavor at home with this New York Strip Steak infused with rich garlic butter. This recipe ensures a perfectly seared exterior and a tender, juicy interior, making every bite a decadent experience. It is surprisingly easy to master, bringing gourmet dining straight to your dinner table.

ingredients.notes: or other high smoke point oil

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove New York Strip steaks from the refrigerator 30-60 minutes before cooking to bring them closer to room temperature. Pat them very dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Heat the Skillet
instructions.text: Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Add the olive oil and heat until it just begins to smoke. Ensure the skillet is very hot before adding the steaks to achieve a good sear.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep brown crust forms. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook one or two at a time if necessary.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Herbs
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the cubed unsalted butter, minced garlic, and fresh rosemary sprigs to the skillet. As the butter melts, tilt the pan and use a large spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the flavorful garlic herb butter for 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking to Desired Doneness
instructions.text: Continue basting and flipping the steaks every minute or so, until they reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 125-130°F; for medium, 130-135°F. Use an instant-read thermometer for accuracy.

instructions.title: Rest the Steaks
instructions.text: Once cooked, transfer the steaks to a cutting board. Pour any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the steaks. Tent loosely with aluminum foil and let them rest for at least 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and juicy steak.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Slice the rested New York Strip steaks against the grain and serve immediately with your favorite side dishes. A final sprinkle of flaky sea salt is optional but highly recommended.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: Always pat your steaks very dry before searing to ensure a perfect crust. Moisture on the surface will steam the meat instead of searing it.
For a deeper flavor, use unsalted butter and control the salt level yourself. Fresh herbs like rosemary or thyme are essential for aromatic basting.
Leftover steak can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently in a skillet or oven to avoid overcooking.
article:
Elevate your weeknight dinner or special occasion with a dish that consistently delivers on flavor and elegance: the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter. This American classic, a staple among beloved American Dinner Recipes, transforms a simple cut of beef into a culinary masterpiece with the irresistible addition of aromatic garlic butter. Imagine a perfectly seared steak, boasting a caramelized crust and a tender, juicy interior, bathed in a rich, herby garlic butter sauce. It’s an experience that rivals any steakhouse, yet is surprisingly achievable in your own kitchen. Forget complicated techniques; with a few key steps and quality ingredients, you can master this impressive meal and delight your taste buds with every succulent bite of New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter.

## Why You'll Love This New York Strip Steak Recipe

There’s an undeniable allure to a perfectly cooked steak, and this New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe captures that magic effortlessly. You'll fall in love with it for several compelling reasons. First and foremost, it brings restaurant-quality dining right to your home. No need for reservations or a hefty bill when you can create a steak that's just as good, if not better, with your own hands. The flavor profile is exceptionally rich and satisfying; the robust, beefy taste of the New York strip is beautifully complemented by the fragrant garlic and luxurious butter, often infused with fresh herbs like rosemary or thyme.

This recipe is also incredibly rewarding to cook. While it looks sophisticated, the process is straightforward, making it accessible even for novice home cooks. It’s a fantastic choice for a celebratory dinner, an intimate date night, or simply when you’re craving something truly special. As one of the quintessential American Dinner Recipes, it’s comforting, familiar, yet utterly gourmet. The crispy, seared exterior gives way to a tender, juicy interior, each bite infused with that incredible garlic butter. It’s a dish that impresses without requiring hours of labor, making it a perfect addition to your culinary repertoire.

## Ingredients for Garlic Butter New York Strip Steak

Crafting the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter starts with selecting high-quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need to create this unforgettable meal:

For the Steaks:
*   **2 New York Strip Steaks:** (1 to 1.5 inches thick, about 12-16 oz each) – Choose good quality, well-marbled steaks for the best flavor and tenderness. Bone-in or boneless works well.
*   **1 tablespoon high smoke point oil:** (such as avocado oil, grapeseed oil, or canola oil) – Essential for searing at high temperatures without burning.
*   **1 teaspoon kosher salt:** (or to taste) – Kosher salt adheres well and provides excellent seasoning.
*   **½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper:** (or to taste) – Freshly ground pepper offers superior flavor.

For the Garlic Butter:
*   **4 tablespoons unsalted butter:** (½ stick) – Unsalted butter allows you to control the overall saltiness of the dish.
*   **4-5 cloves garlic:** (minced) – Fresh garlic is non-negotiable for that potent, aromatic flavor.
*   **2 sprigs fresh rosemary or thyme:** (optional, but highly recommended) – These herbs infuse the butter with an earthy, fragrant note that pairs wonderfully with beef.
*   **Pinch of red pepper flakes:** (optional, for a subtle kick)

Having these components ready before you begin cooking ensures a smooth and enjoyable process, leading to the most delicious New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter you've ever made at home.

## How to Cook New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter

Mastering the art of cooking New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a perfectly seared, juicy steak every time:

1.  **Prepare the Steaks:** Remove the New York strip steaks from the refrigerator about 30-60 minutes before cooking. This allows them to come closer to room temperature, ensuring more even cooking. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels; moisture is the enemy of a good sear. Season both sides generously with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

2.  **Heat the Pan:** Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Let it heat for 3-5 minutes until it’s smoking hot. This high heat is crucial for achieving that beautiful, crusty sear. Add the high smoke point oil to the hot pan and swirl to coat the bottom.

3.  **Sear the Steaks:** Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook one or two at a time if necessary. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep brown crust forms. Use tongs to sear the edges for about 30 seconds each.

4.  **Add Garlic Butter and Baste:** Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the unsalted butter, minced garlic, and fresh herbs (rosemary or thyme) to the pan. As the butter melts, tilt the pan slightly and, using a spoon, continuously baste the melted garlic butter over the steaks for 2-4 minutes. This infuses the steaks with incredible flavor and helps them cook evenly.

5.  **Check for Doneness:** Use an instant-read meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the steak, avoiding the bone, to check for desired doneness:
    *   Rare: 125-130°F (52-54°C)
    *   Medium-Rare: 130-135°F (54-57°C)
    *   Medium: 135-140°F (57-60°C)
    *   Medium-Well: 140-145°F (60-63°C)
    *   Well-Done: 150°F+ (65°C+)
    Remove the steaks from the pan when they are about 5°F below your target temperature, as they will continue to cook while resting.

6.  **Rest the Steaks:** Transfer the steaks to a cutting board. Loosely tent them with aluminum foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This essential step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender and juicy steak.

7.  **Serve:** Slice the steaks against the grain, if desired, and spoon any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the slices. Serve immediately and enjoy your perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter!

## Expert Tips for Perfect Steak

Achieving steakhouse-quality New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Bring Steaks to Room Temperature:** This is crucial. Cold steaks cook unevenly, resulting in a well-done exterior and a rare center. Allowing them to sit out for 30-60 minutes before cooking ensures a more uniform temperature throughout.
*   **Pat Dry, Pat Dry, Pat Dry:** Moisture on the surface of the steak prevents a good sear. Use paper towels to thoroughly dry the steaks before seasoning. A dry surface is key to developing that desirable crispy, brown crust.
*   **Generous Seasoning:** Don't be shy with salt and pepper. These are the primary flavor enhancers for the beef itself. Kosher salt is preferred for its texture and ability to adhere well.
*   **Use a Hot Pan:** A screaming hot pan, ideally cast iron, is non-negotiable for a proper sear (the Maillard reaction). If your pan isn't hot enough, the steak will steam rather than sear, leading to a grey, unappetizing exterior.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** Cooking too many steaks at once will lower the pan's temperature significantly, preventing a good sear. Cook in batches if necessary.
*   **Baste with Purpose:** Once the butter, garlic, and herbs are in the pan, actively baste the steaks. This not only infuses them with flavor but also helps to cook the top surface and edges more evenly.
*   **Invest in a Meat Thermometer:** This is the single most important tool for cooking steak to your desired doneness. Guessing leads to inconsistent results. An instant-read thermometer is worth its weight in gold.
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable:** After cooking, the muscle fibers in the steak are tense, and the juices are concentrated in the center. Resting allows the fibers to relax and the juices to redistribute throughout the entire steak, ensuring every bite is tender and moist. Skip this step at your peril!
*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** Start with good quality New York strip steaks, fresh garlic, and real butter for the best possible outcome.

## What to Serve with Your Steak

A magnificent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter deserves equally delicious accompaniments to complete the meal. When planning your American Dinner Recipes, consider sides that complement the rich flavor of the steak without overpowering it.

For classic steakhouse pairings, consider:
*   **Creamy Mashed Potatoes:** The perfect canvas for soaking up any extra garlic butter.
*   **Roasted Asparagus:** A simple yet elegant green vegetable that provides a nice textural contrast.
*   **Garlic Parmesan Roasted Broccoli:** Roasting brings out the sweetness in broccoli, and the garlic and Parmesan echo the steak's flavors.
*   **Creamed Spinach:** A rich and decadent side that's a steakhouse staple.
*   **Baked Potato:** Loaded with your favorite toppings, it’s a hearty and satisfying choice.

For lighter options:
*   **Simple Green Salad:** A crisp salad with a vinaigrette can cut through the richness of the steak, offering a refreshing contrast.
*   **Sautéed Green Beans with Toasted Almonds:** A quick and flavorful vegetable side.

And to drink? A robust red wine like a Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot would be an excellent choice to pair with the rich flavors of your New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter.

## Storage and Reheating Instructions

While it's hard to imagine having leftovers of such a delicious New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, sometimes it happens! Proper storage and reheating are key to enjoying your steak again without sacrificing too much quality.

**Storage:**
*   Once cooled, transfer any leftover steak to an airtight container.
*   Store in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.

**Reheating:**
Reheating steak can be tricky, as you want to warm it through without overcooking and drying it out. Avoid the microwave if possible, as it tends to make steak rubbery.

*   **Oven Method (Recommended):** Preheat your oven to a low temperature, around 250-275°F (120-135°C). Place the steak on a wire rack set over a baking sheet. Heat for 15-25 minutes, or until just warmed through to your desired temperature. This gentle heat helps the steak warm evenly without drying out.
*   **Skillet Method:** Heat a skillet over medium-low heat. Add a tiny splash of beef broth or water to create some steam, then add the steak. Cover the skillet and heat for a few minutes per side, just until warmed. This method helps retain moisture.

Remember that reheated steak will likely not be as tender or perfectly cooked as when it was fresh, but these methods will yield the best possible results for enjoying your leftovers.

In conclusion, crafting a sensational New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter at home is an incredibly rewarding experience. This American Dinner Recipe staple proves that gourmet meals are well within reach for any home cook. From the initial sear to the luscious garlic butter basting, every step contributes to a dish that is rich, flavorful, and profoundly satisfying. By following these detailed instructions and expert tips, you can confidently create a steak that rivals any steakhouse, impressing your family and friends with your culinary prowess. So, grab your skillet, gather your ingredients, and prepare to indulge in the magnificent taste of a perfectly executed New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter – a true celebration of classic American comfort and elegance.
